title: helianthemum salicifolium subsp. trifoliatum
intro: Helianthemum salicifolium subsp. trifoliatum is a rare, subspecies of the Rockrose plant, characterized by its narrow, willow-like leaves and clusters of pink or white flowers. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and calm digestive issues, making it a popular natural remedy for skin and gut health. Herbal preparations of Helianthemum salicifolium subsp. trifoliatum can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and support overall well-being.
